<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Merging index records**

I'm merging retrieval_fields at top level with node fields for index records. I'm adjusting the chunk filter metadata to handle multiple accessions per chunk with a hard filter on accession = for single filing context. I found that narrative chunks may lack period_years unless from companyfacts merge or document metadata. I discovered that narrative chunks lack period_years except on companyfacts-derived nodes, and finance_metric_exact_keys are high precision for facts chunks but absent on narrative, creating risk of silencing MD&A if applied globally. 

**Analyzing section metadata**

I'm evaluating parser-dependent section paths and metadata heuristics for better chunk classification. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here is a concise report based on `retrieval_fields.py`, `ingestion_service.py`, `sec_company_facts.py`, `chunk_segmenter.py`, and `rag_service.py`.

---

## (1) Current retrieval metadata fields

**Indexed keyword fields** (`RETRIEVAL_INDEX_KEYWORD_FIELDS` in `c:\Users\liuzh\Documents\241201\Backend\rag-testing\src\agent\tools\retrieval_fields.py`):

`schema_version`, `domain`, `content_type`, `language`, `source_section`, `section_leaf`, `section_path`, `section_role`, `leaf_role`, `topic_tags`, `finance_statement`, `finance_period`, `finance_period_confidence`, `finance_metric_keys`, `finance_metric_exact_keys`, `finance_forms`, `finance_form_base`, `finance_accns`, `finance_period_years`, `finance_period_end_dates`

**Indexed text fields:** `search_hints`, `section_path_text`

**Also produced inside `_retrieval_fields` / index records** (and merged on the node payload): `node_type`, `level` (from `NodeRecord`, not in the keyword list but present on records), plus full `metadata` including `_retrieval_fields` blob.

---

## (2) Where they are inferred / built during ingestion

| Source | What gets set |
|--------|----------------|
| **`build_retrieval_fields`** (`retrieval_fields.py`) | Core pipeline: `schema_version`, `domain`, `content_type`, `language`, `source_section`, `section_leaf`, `section_path`, `section_path_text`; for `domain == "finance"`: `section_role`, `leaf_role`, `topic_tags`, `finance_statement`, `finance_period` + `finance_period_confidence`, `finance_metric_keys`; copies **from metadata** (if non-empty lists): `finance_metric_exact_keys`, `finance_forms`, `finance_accns`, `finance_period_years`, `finance_period_end_dates`; derives **`finance_form_base`** from `finance_forms` via `_normalize_finance_form`. Finally `search_hints`. |
| **`_attach_retrieval_fields`** (`ingestion_service.py`) | Called for every node (chunk, section summary, document summary, SEC facts lines) after metadata merge. |
| **`_base_node_metadata` + `_NODE_METADATA_PASSTHROUGH_KEYS`** (`ingestion_service.py`) | Copies document-level keys onto every node: `domain`, `source_kind`, `cik`, `entity_name`, `sec_accession`, `sec_filing_date`, `primary_document`, and the finance filter lists (`finance_forms`, `finance_form_base`, `finance_accns`, `finance_metric_exact_keys`, `finance_period_years`, `finance_period_end_dates`), plus `unstructured_json`. |
| **`build_retrieval_chunks` / `ChunkPayload`** (`chunk_segmenter.py`) | When headings parse: `section_path`, `heading_path`, `section_title`, `section_depth`, page fields. Chunk `title` = section title or `Page {n}`; if neither, ingestion later uses **`Chunk {k}`** as title (`ingestion_service.py` ~462). |
| **`process_edgar_filing_document`** (`ingestion_service.py` ~792+) | Sets **`finance_accns`**, **`finance_forms`**, **`finance_form_base`** (from filing args), merges parser `parse_meta`, then runs normal chunk pipeline. |
| **`build_chunk_filter_metadata`** (`sec_company_facts.py` ~152–197`) | For **companyfacts** line chunks: aggregates distinct **`finance_metric_exact_keys`**, **`finance_forms`**, **`finance_accns`**, **`finance_period_years`**, **`finance_period_end_dates`** from the observation rows in that chunk. |
| **RAG layer** (`rag_service.py` ~819–845) | When narrative evidence is required, **`finance_metric_exact_keys` and `finance_metric_keys` filters are dropped**; may inject **`finance_accns`** from SQL/hypotheses for filing-scoped search. |

---

## (3) Likely stability / quality of named fields

| Field | Stability / quality |
|-----|---------------------|
| **`finance_form_base`** | **High** when sourced from EDGAR ingestion (`form` normalized to e.g. `10-K`). **Moderate** on companyfacts chunks (union of forms in row group). |
| **`finance_accns`** | **High** for filing-scoped HTML (single accession per doc). **Moderate–high** for facts chunks (multiple accessions possible, deduped in order). |
| **`finance_period_years` / `finance_period_end_dates`** | **High** for **companyfacts**-derived chunks (from SEC `period_end` / year slice). **Often missing** on pure narrative HTML nodes unless also present in document passthrough (EDGAR path in code does not add them to `doc_meta`). |
| **`finance_metric_exact_keys`** | **High** for facts chunks (real XBRL `metric_key`s). **Usually absent** on narrative chunks — aligns with intentional filter drop for narrative. |
| **`section_role`** | **Heuristic** (rules over path + title + ~1200 chars of text, or explicit `metadata.section_role`). Frequent **`unknown`**. |
| **`leaf_role`** | **Heuristic**, depends on `section_role` + markers (`infer_finance_leaf_role`). Many **`other`** / **`mda_intro`**. |
| **`topic_tags`** | **Heuristic** small tag set (`_TOPIC_TAG_RULES`) or explicit list; coverage is limited. |
| **`section_path`** | **Good** when headings exist. **Poor** when no headings: falls back to title → often **`["Chunk N"]`** (see `chunk_segmenter` + `Chunk {chunk_order + 1}` in `ingestion_service.py`). |
| **`finance_statement`** | **Heuristic** — first label match in pooled source text (`_FINANCE_STATEMENT_LABELS`). Often **null** outside obvious statement headings. |
| **`content_type`** | **Reliable** for structural types (`document_summary`, `section_summary` from `node_type`). **Heuristic** for body text (`financial_note`, `financial_statement`, `finance_chunk`, etc.). |

---

## (4) Risks as **hard** filters (vs soft / rerank)

| Field | Hard-filter risk |
|-------|-------------------|
| **`finance_form_base` / `finance_forms`** | Can be appropriate to restrict to 10-Q vs 10-K; risk if normalization or amended filings (`/A` stripped) don’t match user intent, or facts chunks span multiple forms. |
| **`finance_accns`** | Strong for “this filing only”; **dangerous** for cross-filing questions if you require a single accession. Good match to how `rag_service` injects accns for narrative. |
| **`finance_period_years` / `finance_period_end_dates`** | Safe for **facts** retrieval when aligned with SQL period; **excluding all narrative** if applied broadly to mixed corpora where those fields are empty on HTML nodes. |
| **`finance_metric_exact_keys`** | **Very risky** on mixed index: narrative lacks these — already mitigated by dropping when `need_narrative`. Hard filter = silent misses for prose. |
| **`section_role`** | **High false negative risk** (`unknown` and misclassification; rules are not exhaustive). Better as **boost** than required match. |
| **`leaf_role`** | Same, plus noisy labels (`boilerplate` vs substantive). **Soft prior** preferred. |
| **`topic_tags`** | Incomplete tags; keyword rules miss paraphrases. **Soft**. |
| **`section_path`** | Brittle when path is `Chunk N` or parser-specific; exact match filters out valid chunks. **Soft** or fuzzy. |
| **`finance_statement`** | Single-label, first-match heuristic; many chunks **null**. **Soft** only. |
| **`content_type`** | **Moderate**: filtering `document_summary` / `section_summary` vs `chunk` is structurally meaningful; finer `finance_chunk` vs `financial_statement` is still heuristic — use with care. |

**Practical split (aligned with this codebase):** Treat **document identity** fields (`finance_accns`, often `finance_form_base`) as the strongest hard filters when the user or planner pins a filing or form. Treat **XBRL-derived period/metric lists** as hard filters primarily on **facts** paths. Treat **all inferred narrative semantics** (`section_role`, `leaf_role`, `topic_tags`, `finance_statement`, weak `finance_period` / `finance_metric_keys`) as **soft signals** or rerank features unless you accept recall loss. The existing `rag_service` behavior (dropping metric filters for narrative) matches that split.
